channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Anthropic's Mythos Model Found Vulnerabilities In Classified US Government Systems

media:content.media:description: US administration directed Anthropic to prevent foreign nationals from using Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

item.description: A official said Anthropic had teamed up with US intelligence agencies to conduct tests using the company's Mythos model.
item.content:encoded: A official said Anthropic had teamed up with US intelligence agencies to conduct tests using the company's Mythos model.

item.title: "Going To Happen": UN Watchdog Chief On Inspection Of Iran's Nuclear Sites

media:content.media:description: The IAEA has been allowed to visit other nuclear sites in Iran since the 12-day war in 2025.

item.description: The comment by International Atomic Energy Agency head Rafael Mariano Grossi was the firmest yet from the United Nations agency, which is viewed as key in determining the status of Iran's nuclear stockpile.
item.content:encoded: The comment by International Atomic Energy Agency head Rafael Mariano Grossi was the firmest yet from the United Nations agency, which is viewed as key in determining the status of Iran's nuclear stockpile.

item.title: Trump, JD Vance Call Iran Deal A Payday For US Farmers, Tehran Denies It

media:content.media:description: Lashing back at critics on his Truth Social media platform, Trump said US farmers would get a payday.

item.description: Donald Trump and JD Vance say that any frozen funds and assets held outside of Iran will be used to buy US crops.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump and JD Vance say that any frozen funds and assets held outside of Iran will be used to buy US crops.

item.title: No Kennedy In Congress Next Year As Jack Schlossberg Loses New York Race

media:content.media:description: Jack Schlossberg got plenty of attention in the race.

item.description: The campaign was colourful and hotly contested, partly because of Schlossberg's star power as the social-media-savvy grandson of the late President John F Kennedy, but also because the race became an expensive proxy fight among artificial intelligence interests.
item.content:encoded: The campaign was colourful and hotly contested, partly because of Schlossberg's star power as the social-media-savvy grandson of the late President John F Kennedy, but also because the race became an expensive proxy fight among artificial intelligence interests.

item.title: Barnacles, Algae And Delays: The Cleanup Challenge As Hormuz Gradually Reopens

media:content.media:description: After spending months anchored in warm Gulf waters, many vessels now need extensive underwater cleaning

item.description: Barnacles, algae and mussels are creating fresh delays for ships waiting to resume journeys through one of the world's busiest oil routes
item.content:encoded: Barnacles, algae and mussels are creating fresh delays for ships waiting to resume journeys through one of the world's busiest oil routes

item.title: SpaceX Sells $25 Billion In Bonds To Cut Elon Musk's Interest Burden

media:content.media:description: Demand for SpaceX debt suggests that many investors have faith.

item.description: Ahead of SpaceX's historic $75 billion initial public offering earlier this month, Elon Musk fused many of his disparate companies into a conglomerate that investors gushed over, even though it doesn't yet make a profit.
item.content:encoded: Ahead of SpaceX's historic $75 billion initial public offering earlier this month, Elon Musk fused many of his disparate companies into a conglomerate that investors gushed over, even though it doesn't yet make a profit.

item.title: Legal Tech Firm Sues US Over Order Limiting Foreign Access To Top-Tier Anthropic Models

media:content.media:description: Anthropic and the United States are locked in legal battles in Washington and California federal courts

item.description: Legion LegalTech asked a U.S. judge to vacate and set aside the administration's directive targeting Anthropic.
item.content:encoded: Legion LegalTech asked a U.S. judge to vacate and set aside the administration's directive targeting Anthropic.

item.title: "Indians Won't Do That": Trump On India As Ukraine Peacekeeper Force Plan

media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump, Prime Minister Narendra Modi (File)

item.description: Donald Trump reportedly shot down a plan to use Indian troops as a peacekeeping force in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump reportedly shot down a plan to use Indian troops as a peacekeeping force in Ukraine.

item.title: Police Find Drugs In Bag Labelled 'Definitely Not A Bag Full Of Drugs', 2 Arrested

media:content.media:description: Suspected narcotics and drug paraphernalia found in an ironically labelled bag led to two arrests.

item.description: Kentucky deputy arrests two after discovering suspected drugs in a bag labelled 'definitely not a bag full of drugs'.
item.content:encoded: Kentucky deputy arrests two after discovering suspected drugs in a bag labelled 'definitely not a bag full of drugs'.

item.title: Women Drugged, Raped, Stabbed As France's Annual Music Night Turns Deadly

media:content.media:description: Partygoers gather during the annual street music festival 'Fete de la Musique' in Paris.

item.description: The annual Fete de la Musique festival in France descended into widespread violence and anarchy, resulting in 243 arrests nationwide, multiple stabbings, and severe sexual assaults, including instances of women being targeted with needle spikes.
item.content:encoded: The annual Fete de la Musique festival in France descended into widespread violence and anarchy, resulting in 243 arrests nationwide, multiple stabbings, and severe sexual assaults, including instances of women being targeted with needle spikes.

item.title: US-Iran War Live: US "Very Naive" On Iran's Nuclear Programme, Tel Aviv To Act Alone: Israel
item.description: US-Iran War Peace Deal Live News: It is Israel's responsibility to confront this Iranian threat and act against it alone, said Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War Peace Deal Live News: It is Israel's responsibility to confront this Iranian threat and act against it alone, said Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

item.title: What Is Driving Europe's Heatwave?

media:content.media:description: Scientists say heatwaves are made more intense in a world hotter because of burning fossil fuels.

item.description: Europe Heatwave: Scientists say there has been an increase in high pressure systems in Europe in recent decades but whether this is a consequence of climate change remains a subject of debate.
item.content:encoded: Europe Heatwave: Scientists say there has been an increase in high pressure systems in Europe in recent decades but whether this is a consequence of climate change remains a subject of debate.

item.title: Only 24% Of Americans Think War With Iran Was Worth It: Polls

media:content.media:description: Some 63% of Americans think it unlikely that the deal Trump signed will lead to lasting peace.

item.description: Only 23% of Americans — including just half of Republicans — think the US is now in a stronger position with Iran compared with its position before the war, the poll found.
item.content:encoded: Only 23% of Americans — including just half of Republicans — think the US is now in a stronger position with Iran compared with its position before the war, the poll found.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Unveils Plans For 10,000-Ton Warships, Nuclear Navy

media:content.media:description: Kim Jong Un speaks at the Second Plenary Meeting of the Workers' Party of Korea in Pyongyang.

item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his country was "equipping the Navy with nuclear weapons" as he commissioned a warship, state media reported on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his country was "equipping the Navy with nuclear weapons" as he commissioned a warship, state media reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Would "Cancel Meetings" With Iran If UN Nuclear Inspections Denied: Trump

media:content.media:description: Trump suggested that Tehran's position had been significantly weakened.

item.description: Responding to questions on UN nuclear watchdog inspectors would be deployed under the agreement, Trump insisted that Iran had already agreed in principle.
item.content:encoded: Responding to questions on UN nuclear watchdog inspectors would be deployed under the agreement, Trump insisted that Iran had already agreed in principle.

item.title: Trump Claims Record 19 Million Barrels Of Oil Transited Hormuz; Prices Ease

media:content.media:description: Oil prices are tumbling down, and the world is a much safer place," Trump wrote on Truth Social.

item.description: Oil prices ticked lower Tuesday with Brent crude futures, the international benchmark, fell 45 cents to USD 77.45 a barrel. West Texas Intermediate futures declined 34 cents to USD 73.52.
item.content:encoded: Oil prices ticked lower Tuesday with Brent crude futures, the international benchmark, fell 45 cents to USD 77.45 a barrel. West Texas Intermediate futures declined 34 cents to USD 73.52.

item.title: Trains Halted Across Germany After Radio System Outage

media:content.media:description: It said that all trains were being held at stations because of an issue with a digital radio system.

item.description: Germany's national railway operator said it halted trains across the country on Tuesday evening because of a problem with a communication system.
item.content:encoded: Germany's national railway operator said it halted trains across the country on Tuesday evening because of a problem with a communication system.

item.title: US Senate Votes To Halt Iran War In Rebuke To Trump

media:content.media:description: The Senate vote was nearly along party lines, with 4 Republicans joining all but one Democrat in favor.

item.description: The US Senate backed legislation on Tuesday directing President Donald Trump to halt US military action against Iran, the latest rebuke of the Republican president from an increasingly restive Congress.
item.content:encoded: The US Senate backed legislation on Tuesday directing President Donald Trump to halt US military action against Iran, the latest rebuke of the Republican president from an increasingly restive Congress.

item.title: Marco Rubio Rejects Iran Tolls On Hormuz As Deal Strains Multiply

media:content.media:description: Rubio, opening a regional tour in the United Arab Emirates, said Washington would oppose any such move.

item.description: Iran War: An Iranian blockade early in the war choked maritime traffic through the strait, sending global oil prices surging, but crossings have begun rising since the deal was signed.
item.content:encoded: Iran War: An Iranian blockade early in the war choked maritime traffic through the strait, sending global oil prices surging, but crossings have begun rising since the deal was signed.

item.title: Alibaba Sues US For Being Linking It To Chinese Military

media:content.media:description: "The determinations have no basis in fact or law," Alibaba said.

item.description: Alibaba, the Chinese technology and e-commerce giant, sued the US government on Tuesday over being placed on a list of businesses from China that the Department of Defense linked to that country's military.
item.content:encoded: Alibaba, the Chinese technology and e-commerce giant, sued the US government on Tuesday over being placed on a list of businesses from China that the Department of Defense linked to that country's military.
